So a bunch of Reddit degenerates with diamond hands managed to obliterate a hedge fund that survived the 2008 financial crisis. Melvin Capital, worth billions, gets absolutely demolished by people who call themselves 'apes' and communicate primarily through rocket ship emojis.

The SEC suddenly discovers that maybe, just maybe, hedge funds should tell people when they're betting against companies into oblivion. It only took a complete market meltdown and congressional hearings for regulators to realize transparency might be important.

Robinhood gets slapped with a $65 million fine for 'misleading customers' during their convenient trading halt. Apparently telling people they're protecting them while only protecting Wall Street billionaires counts as misleading, who knew.

The meme stock contagion spreads faster than a TikTok dance trend, turning dying movie theaters and forgotten phone companies into lottery tickets. Professional investors watch in horror as fundamentals become completely irrelevant and stonks only go up.

r/WallStreetBets explodes from a niche gambling addiction support group to 8 million strong overnight. Suddenly everyone's an expert on gamma squeezes and short interest, armed with Robinhood accounts and stimulus checks.

Every trading app simultaneously crashes because apparently nobody planned for millions of people to actually use their financial services at once. Modern technology meets the raw power of retail FOMO and loses spectacularly.

And then Chipotle, CHIPOTLE, has the absolute audacity to blame a fifty cent burrito price hike on 'market volatility insurance.' ARE YOU KIDDING ME? Your guac costs extra because of GameStop? This is where I draw the line, people!
